name: ExtraSpace Storage Scholarships for Undergraduate & Graduate
description: ExtraSpace Storage awards scholarships to full-time college students pursuing undergraduate or graduate degrees. This scholarship supports students who demonstrate academic achievement and a commitment to their education.
eligibility: Full-time U.S. college student over 16 years of age
applicationRequirements: Online application, proof of enrollment as a full-time student

name: Addictions.com Essay Contest
description: Addictions.com sponsors an essay contest to raise awareness about addiction and recovery. Students write about topics related to substance abuse, mental health, and recovery to compete for scholarship funds.
eligibility: Full-time U.S. college student over 18 with a minimum 2.5 GPA
applicationRequirements: Essay submission on an addiction-related topic, transcript showing 2.5+ GPA

name: Minecraft Scholarship
description: The Minecraft Scholarship is awarded to a college student who demonstrates creativity and passion for gaming. Applicants share how Minecraft has influenced their creativity, problem-solving skills, or education.
eligibility: Full-time U.S. college student with a minimum 3.0 GPA
applicationRequirements: Online application, essay about how Minecraft has impacted your life, transcript showing 3.0+ GPA

name: Bryan Cameron Education Foundation
description: The Bryan Cameron Education Foundation provides full-ride scholarships covering tuition, room, board, and additional expenses for exceptional students. Cameron Scholars also receive mentoring, leadership development, and networking opportunities throughout college.
eligibility: Current high school junior planning to enroll at a four-year institution with a minimum 3.7 GPA
applicationRequirements: Online application, transcript showing 3.7+ GPA, letters of recommendation, personal essays, interview

name: The Mikkelson Foundation Student Scholarship Program
description: The Mikkelson Foundation supports Colorado seniors pursuing degrees in engineering, mathematics, or the sciences. This renewable scholarship helps offset the cost of a four-year college education for high-achieving STEM students.
eligibility: Colorado high school senior studying engineering, math, or sciences with a minimum 3.7 GPA and 1200 SAT score
applicationRequirements: Online application, transcript showing 3.7+ GPA, SAT scores (1200+), essay, letter of recommendation

name: Coca-Cola Scholars Program
description: The Coca-Cola Scholars Program is one of the largest corporate-sponsored scholarship programs in the United States. It recognizes 150 high school seniors annually for their leadership, academic excellence, and commitment to community service.
eligibility: High school senior with a minimum 3.0 GPA and strong record of community involvement
applicationRequirements: Online application, transcript, list of extracurricular activities and community service, essays

name: Daniels Fund Scholarship
description: The Daniels Fund Scholarship provides comprehensive funding for students in Colorado, New Mexico, Utah, and Wyoming to attend any accredited nonprofit college or university in the U.S. Scholars receive mentoring and support throughout their college journey.
eligibility: High school senior in Colorado, New Mexico, Utah, or Wyoming demonstrating financial need, academic promise, and strong character
applicationRequirements: Online application, FAFSA, transcript, letters of recommendation, personal essays, interview

name: Boettcher Foundation Scholarship
description: The Boettcher Scholarship is Colorado's most prestigious merit-based scholarship, awarded to approximately 42 students each year. It covers full tuition, fees, a book allowance, and a living stipend at any Colorado college or university.
eligibility: Colorado high school senior with outstanding academics, leadership, service, and character. Must attend a Colorado institution.
applicationRequirements: Online application, transcript, ACT/SAT scores, letters of recommendation, essays, community involvement, interview

name: The Gates Scholarship
description: The Gates Scholarship is a highly selective, full-cost-of-attendance scholarship for exceptional, Pell-eligible minority high school seniors. Funded by the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation, it covers any costs not already covered by financial aid.
eligibility: Pell-eligible minority high school senior with a minimum 3.3 GPA. Must be African American, American Indian, Asian/Pacific Islander, or Hispanic American.
applicationRequirements: Online application, FAFSA, CSS Profile, transcript, letters of recommendation, essays

name: Ron Brown Scholar Program
description: The Ron Brown Scholar Program awards scholarships to talented and economically disadvantaged African American high school seniors who demonstrate academic excellence, leadership, and community service.
eligibility: African American high school senior demonstrating financial need, academic excellence, and leadership
applicationRequirements: Online application, transcript, SAT/ACT scores, financial information, essays, letters of recommendation, interview

name: Jack Kent Cooke College Scholarship
description: The Jack Kent Cooke Foundation College Scholarship Program is one of the largest scholarships in the country for high-achieving students with financial need. It supports students attending the nation's top colleges and universities.
eligibility: High school senior with a minimum 3.5 GPA, family income under $95,000, and strong standardized test scores
applicationRequirements: Online application, transcript, SAT/ACT scores, FAFSA, tax returns, letters of recommendation, essays

name: Colorado First Generation Scholarship
description: This scholarship supports first-generation college students from Colorado who are breaking barriers by being the first in their family to attend college. Recipients demonstrate resilience, academic potential, and commitment to their community.
eligibility: Colorado high school senior who is the first in their immediate family to attend a four-year college. Minimum 2.5 GPA.
applicationRequirements: Online application, transcript, essay about being a first-generation student, letter of recommendation

name: National Merit Scholarship Program
description: The National Merit Scholarship Program honors high school students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. Students enter by taking the PSAT/NMSQT during their junior year and progressing through semifinalist and finalist stages.
eligibility: High school junior who takes the PSAT/NMSQT and qualifies based on state selection index scores
applicationRequirements: PSAT/NMSQT score, online application (for finalists), transcript, SAT scores, essay, letter of recommendation

name: Scholastic Art & Writing Awards
description: The Scholastic Art & Writing Awards is the nation's longest-running recognition program for creative teens. Students in grades 7-12 submit original works of art and writing for the chance to earn scholarships, exhibition, and publication.
eligibility: Students in grades 9-12 who submit original art or writing
applicationRequirements: Portfolio submission of original artwork or writing through the online portal

name: Hispanic Heritage Foundation Youth Awards
description: The Hispanic Heritage Foundation Youth Awards recognize Hispanic high school seniors who excel in the classroom, show leadership in their communities, and celebrate their Hispanic heritage. Awards are given in multiple categories including STEM, education, and healthcare.
eligibility: Hispanic/Latino high school senior with a minimum 3.0 GPA
applicationRequirements: Online application, transcript, essay, extracurricular activities list

name: Elks Most Valuable Student Scholarship
description: The Elks National Foundation awards Most Valuable Student scholarships to high school seniors who demonstrate leadership, scholarship, and financial need. The top awards are $50,000 over four years.
eligibility: High school senior who is a U.S. citizen. Based on scholarship, leadership, and financial need.
applicationRequirements: Online application, transcript, SAT/ACT scores, financial information, list of activities, essay

name: Colorado STEM Academy Scholarship
description: The Colorado STEM Academy Scholarship supports Colorado high school students who are passionate about science, technology, engineering, and mathematics. Preference is given to students from underrepresented backgrounds in STEM fields.
eligibility: Colorado high school student in grades 10-12 with a minimum 3.0 GPA and demonstrated interest in STEM
applicationRequirements: Online application, transcript, essay about STEM goals, letter of recommendation from a STEM teacher

name: CHSAA Athlete of Character Scholarship
description: The Colorado High School Activities Association awards the Athlete of Character Scholarship to student-athletes who demonstrate sportsmanship, integrity, and academic achievement while excelling in their sport.
eligibility: Colorado high school junior or senior who participates in CHSAA-sanctioned sports with a minimum 3.0 GPA
applicationRequirements: Online application, transcript, coach recommendation, essay on character and sportsmanship

name: QuestBridge National College Match
description: QuestBridge connects high-achieving students from low-income backgrounds with full four-year scholarships to the nation's most selective colleges. Matched students receive binding full-ride scholarships covering tuition, room, board, books, and travel.
eligibility: High school senior from a low-income household (typically under $65,000 for a family of four) with outstanding academic record
applicationRequirements: QuestBridge application, transcript, SAT/ACT scores, tax returns, essays, letters of recommendation, school report

name: Denver Scholarship Foundation Future Fund Scholarship
description: The Denver Scholarship Foundation inspires and empowers Denver Public Schools students to enroll in and graduate from college. The Future Fund Scholarship provides renewable financial support for up to five years of postsecondary education.
eligibility: Denver Public Schools senior with financial need. Must complete FAFSA and college counseling milestones.
applicationRequirements: Online application through DSF portal, FAFSA completion, participation in college counseling, transcript
